vol_name: Do embodiment and fictionality affect young children’s learning?
vol_description: Preschool children aged 3-5 are randomly assigned to 1) read stories 2) play with puppets 3) play in costume. During the play/stories they are given information regarding reality status. They are then tested for fiction/reality reasoning and memory for facts presented within the stories.
